headline: From prime regularity exploration to generator-fingerprint research tooling.

change_dashboard.headline: PrimeProject moved from exploratory prime regularity visuals into publication-gated real-world generator fingerprint tooling.

maturity_ladder.signal: controlled attribution, null calibration, 8-setting replication audit, and scoped classifier baseline
OpenSSL/BoringSSL/Go/Bitcoin collection targets, sample-power floors, handoff, submission contract, fixture audit, pre-intake lint, and intake validation
provenance, acceptance, promotion, handoff, submission-contract, lint-fixture, and intake gates before claims
5 falsification checks, 6 consistency checks, and controlled-synthetic-only claim floor

visual_rollup.headline: Visible change history: exploration scale, controlled signal, sim-to-real gates, and publication guardrails.

release_trail.title: Prime regularity demo
release_trail.proof: interactive gap, residue, and next-prime measure views

release_trail.title: Scale lift
release_trail.proof: larger local runs are visible on GitHub Pages without recomputation

release_trail.title: Controlled attribution
release_trail.proof: signal must survive bit-length control, null calibration, and replication

release_trail.title: Sim-to-real gates
release_trail.proof: OpenSSL/BoringSSL/Go/Bitcoin baselines are registered but not accepted

release_trail.title: Publication guardrails
release_trail.proof: claim-language audit, claim ledger, lineage, decision protocol, falsification battery, and consistency audit prevent overclaiming

release_trail.title: Proof route pruning
release_trail.measure: 5/5 scalar clause-rank grammars refuted at 28 bits
release_trail.proof: TICKET-46 turns the Collatz scalar-rank route into a restricted no-go result, not a conjecture proof

release_trail.title: Stateful lasso pruning
release_trail.proof: TICKET-47 extracts a 16-edge positive-debt lasso and blocks zero-memory through last-4 edge-memory repairs

release_trail.title: Automaton reachability split
release_trail.proof: TICKET-48 blocks fixed finite total state repairs over the abstract lasso and isolates concrete reachability as the next theorem

release_trail.title: Preimage obstruction isolated
release_trail.proof: TICKET-49 identifies the first local coordinate that blocks the concrete Collatz lasso prefix

release_trail.title: Phase-lift exception promoted
release_trail.proof: TICKET-50 refutes the local all-phase obstruction and promotes two near-lasso witnesses instead

release_trail.title: Terminal lift closed
release_trail.proof: TICKET-51 closes both depth-15 near-lasso ancestries at phase 15 without claiming Collatz

release_trail.title: 48-bit frontier exposed
release_trail.proof: TICKET-52 finds and terminally closes one new 48-bit near-lasso witness while proving blind enumeration is no longer viable

release_trail.title: Terminal theorem isolated
release_trail.measure: 3 roots / high branch v=9 / 0 terminal matches
release_trail.proof: TICKET-53 refutes the extracted phase-15 lasso family by a symbolic low/high terminal mismatch theorem

release_trail.title: Next family extracted
release_trail.measure: 69,090 remaining starts / max depth 5 / 4,372 phase-5 gates
release_trail.proof: TICKET-54 removes the terminal family and identifies Phase5ValuationGate as the strongest remaining bounded Collatz family

release_trail.title: Gate tunnel closed
release_trail.measure: 3 gate crossers / 3 tunnels / 0 terminal target matches
release_trail.proof: TICKET-55 proves the extracted phase-5 gate crossers tunnel into the TICKET53 terminal no-go

release_trail.title: Projection escape isolated
release_trail.proof: TICKET-56 closes the exact 32-bit lasso partition but refutes simple projection-closure globalization

release_trail.title: Boundary state obstruction
release_trail.proof: TICKET-57 shows exact32 outcomes need affine boundary coordinates and finds no full-period replay among known near-lasso roots

release_trail.title: Lift stability refuted
release_trail.proof: TICKET-58 replays the deterministic 48-bit sample and refutes unchanged exact32 affine-boundary lifting

release_trail.title: Cylinder coordinate gap
release_trail.proof: TICKET-59 turns sampled lift mismatches into counted low40-to-48 cylinders and shows low40 is still not a complete coordinate

release_trail.title: Failure-offset separator
release_trail.measure: 58 mixed cylinders / 210 lifts / first joint separator
release_trail.proof: TICKET-60 finds low40 + failure_offset separates selected mixed cylinders but remains replay-derived

release_trail.title: Pre-replay separator
release_trail.measure: mod 16 first joint separator / top 6 bits also closes
release_trail.proof: TICKET-61 replaces replay-derived failure_offset with low40 + high_extension mod 16 on the selected mixed-cylinder population

release_trail.title: Mod16 lift survival
release_trail.proof: TICKET-62 tests higher lifts and finds low40 + base mod16 still deterministic on surviving start-template lifts

release_trail.title: Mod16 automaton table
release_trail.proof: TICKET-63 extracts a deterministic automaton table and reduces the 60-bit quotient to low40 mod 2^20 + base_mod16

release_trail.title: Symbolic gate obstruction
release_trail.proof: TICKET-64 rejects direct symbolic promotion and replaces it with a gate-plus-offset transition target

release_trail.title: Start-template chain extinction
release_trail.proof: TICKET-65 closes the tracked start-template chain by 80 bits while showing compact gate separators collapse to row-unique states

release_trail.title: Complement frontier isolated
release_trail.proof: TICKET-66 refutes the already-covered complement shortcut and turns the next target into a rank theorem or compatible infinite lift

release_trail.title: Rank cycle frontier
release_trail.proof: TICKET-67 refutes one-step split and simple rank closure while isolating the cyclic quotient that must be refined next

release_trail.title: Refined DAG frontier
release_trail.proof: TICKET-68 breaks the observed 429-node quotient cycle under prefix/consumed refinement; tail/residue-only refinement still leaves 26 cyclic nodes and transition-completeness remains open

release_trail.title: Rank frontier audit
release_trail.proof: TICKET-69 validates observed prefix/consumed rank descent and isolates the unexpanded rank-0 frontier that blocks a proof

release_trail.title: Frontier closure refuted
release_trail.proof: TICKET-70 expands the rank-0 frontier and refutes the shortcut that those states are automatically terminal sinks

release_trail.title: Bounded separator tradeoff
release_trail.measure: 0 mixed full-word keys / 22,219 compact mixed keys
release_trail.proof: TICKET-71 finds a bounded full-word separator but shows the compact coordinate still needs an infinite lift-closure theorem

release_trail.title: Persistent lift-chain pressure
release_trail.proof: TICKET-72 separates rank descent from open pressure and shows compact mixed-key pressure re-enters under lift

release_trail.title: Strict re-entry tree exhausted
release_trail.proof: TICKET-73 exactly exhausts the selected strict re-entry predicate at the fifth lift; the all-trajectory coverage theorem remains open

release_trail.title: Coverage leakage exposed
release_trail.proof: TICKET-74 refutes promotion of strict-tree extinction to coverage: fifth-lift pressure leaves the old cover and persists through a sixth lift

release_trail.title: Finite-coordinate closure blocked
release_trail.proof: TICKET-75 quantifies the Collatz compression-versus-state-growth obstruction: coarse coordinates retain cycles and mixed outcomes, while rich coordinates leak into unseen classes

release_trail.title: Boundary recurrence isolated
release_trail.measure: 297,104 transitions / 0 formula failures / 5 fixed precisions refuted
release_trail.proof: TICKET-76 derives the exact four-bit boundary quotient recurrence and shows that unresolved normalization exposes four new bits at each lift

release_trail.title: Fixed-prefix ghost classified
release_trail.measure: 18,569 equality boundaries / max 15 strict steps / 0 failures
release_trail.proof: Corrected TICKET-77 classifies the inverse-16 orbit modulo 3^(m+1), rolls equality valuations back, and identifies each all-depth fixed-prefix completion as a non-natural 2-adic preimage of -1/3

release_trail.title: Finite 2-adic separator blocked
release_trail.proof: TICKET-78 proves every finite accelerated valuation cylinder contains infinitely many positive integers, so a fixed finite 2-adic state cannot certify natural admissibility

release_trail.title: Bounded coupled one-step rank blocked
release_trail.proof: TICKET-79 uses exact natural expansion and nonterminal contraction families to refute alpha*log(n) plus any bounded finite-state correction as a universal one-step Collatz rank

release_trail.title: Finite least-counterexample compactness blocked
release_trail.proof: TICKET-80 proves every finite non-descent prefix system has arbitrarily large natural witnesses while its nested all-ones cylinders converge 2-adically to the nonpositive fixed point -1

release_trail.title: Single Mersenne compensation blocked
release_trail.proof: TICKET-81 exactly classifies the first compensation after a stabilized Mersenne expansion block: only k=2,4,8 descend, while infinite odd and even families do not

release_trail.title: Every fixed Mersenne window blocked
release_trail.proof: TICKET-82 constructs explicit exponent progressions with arbitrarily long post-expansion non-descent prefixes, proving unbounded Mersenne stopping delay without claiming divergence

release_trail.title: Sub-half-log Mersenne windows blocked
release_trail.proof: TICKET-83 proves D(k_H)>H>(1/2)log2(k_H)-2 on an explicit infinite exponent sequence, ruling out every o(log k) and coefficient-below-1/2 descent window

release_trail.title: Two-thirds log window blocked
release_trail.proof: TICKET-84 lifts finite prefixes of the accessible -7/-5 two-adic valuation cycle to positive exponents and proves D(k_H)>H>(2/3)log2(k_H)-1 without treating the ghost as natural

release_trail.title: Accessible cycle coefficient supremum solved
release_trail.proof: TICKET-85 proves the accessible periodic-word reciprocal-mean supremum is one, constructs D(k_H)>log2(k_H)-2, and excludes attainment via the all-ones mod-8 obstruction

release_trail.title: Coefficient-one zero-additive boundary crossed
release_trail.proof: TICKET-86 reduces the target to 3^(r_H+1)=-7 modulo 2^(H+3), proves infinitely many top-bit heights by non-stabilization, and obtains D(r_H)>log2(r_H) on an infinite Mersenne subsequence

release_trail.title: Additive-one Mersenne boundary crossed
release_trail.measure: 262,143 bits / 65,368 positive runs / max run 16 / 0 failures
release_trail.proof: TICKET-87 proves both lift digits recur infinitely, converts infinitely many one-to-zero transitions into D(k)>log2(k)+1, and keeps the observed length-16 run as finite evidence only

release_trail.title: Run-length-two shortcut rejected
release_trail.proof: TICKET-88 gives an aperiodic no-00 countermodel and proves the natural complement exponent enters valuation cycle (1,3), isolating FixedLogGoldenMeanExclusion as the missing arithmetic bridge

release_trail.title: Golden-mean target reduced to valuation excess
release_trail.proof: TICKET-89 proves 100 recurrence is exactly recurrence of v2(3^(k+1)+7)-floor(log2(k))>=5 and rules out transcendence or positive-entropy counting as standalone bridges

release_trail.title: Fixed-precision error automata blocked
release_trail.proof: TICKET-90 derives the normalized-error recurrence and proves the limiting odd beta fixed point survives in every fixed modular precision

release_trail.title: Single ghost replaced by full invariant set
release_trail.measure: 255 horizons / 4,096 conjugacy states / 377 no-00 words / 0 failures
release_trail.proof: TICKET-91 proves growing-precision error-tail conjugacy, identifies beta with the all-one tail, and replaces single-point separation by escape from the full golden-mean invariant set

release_trail.title: Target scales corrected before promotion
release_trail.measure: 131,306 Collatz jumps / 17 Twin gap promotions removed / 0 failures
release_trail.proof: TICKET-92 preserves Collatz's constant additive digit defect and corrects TP-TICKET-14: Maynard's criterion uses ceil(theta M_k/2), so legacy 2/k surrogate scores certify neither M_k nor gap 2

release_trail.title: Twin target moved to exact correlation excess
release_trail.measure: 2M exact correlation / 14,871 twins / 4 surrogate countermodels / 0 failures
release_trail.proof: TICKET-93 proves that unbounded Lambda shift-two correlation excess over explicit prime-power contamination implies twin infinitude, and refutes positive truncated-divisor correlation as an uncertified lower bound

release_trail.title: Signed remainder budget exposed
release_trail.measure: 4 Twin norm bounds blocked / 4 Goldbach checkpoints / 0 failures
release_trail.proof: TICKET-94 proves the exact Twin residual identity, shows norm-only lower bounds stay negative, and gives a per-even Goldbach correlation-excess bridge after prime-power contamination

release_trail.title: Equivalent target downgraded, contamination sharpened
release_trail.measure: 1M Goldbach screen / 10 small criterion exceptions / 4 Twin equivalence checks / 0 failures
release_trail.proof: TICKET-95 replaces exponent-count contamination by weighted proper-prime-power mass, proves the TICKET-94 Twin residual target is an equivalent reformulation rather than a reduction, and keeps the million-scale FFT result explicitly numerical

release_trail.title: Fourier phase information isolated
release_trail.measure: 2 scales / 36 masks each / 0 sparse energy certificates / 0 replay failures
release_trail.proof: TICKET-96 reconstructs Goldbach and shift-two correlations as exact finite DFT coefficients, then blocks phase-blind minor-energy routes with explicit information countermodels

release_trail.title: Fixed-modulus arithmetic information exhausted
release_trail.measure: 2 scales / 5 optimal moduli / 0 correlation certificates / 0 contract failures
release_trail.proof: TICKET-97 uses the L2-optimal residue-periodic projection through modulus 2,310 and proves by an explicit zero-residue-mean countermodel that fixed-modulus one-point information does not determine binary correlation signs

release_trail.title: Growing-modulus data leakage isolated
release_trail.measure: 3 scales / 8 primorial moduli / 0 pre-leak certificates / 0 contract failures
release_trail.proof: TICKET-98 proves that W at least the dataset length makes the fitted periodic projection identical to the observations, and finds every first Goldbach/Twin norm certificate exactly at this row-unique replay boundary

release_trail.title: Target-independent local model established
release_trail.measure: 120 cross-fit configurations / 0 norm certificates / 1M residual holdout / 0 contract failures
release_trail.proof: TICKET-99 separates training from evaluation, proves a CRT lower bound for the external coprime local main, and promotes only the still-open signed residual decay theorem while keeping K=1.6 as finite evidence

release_trail.title: Joint Vaughan cancellation isolated
release_trail.measure: 6M Goldbach / 10M Twin / Type II counterexample K=7.91 / 0 contract failures
release_trail.proof: TICKET-100 extends the K=1.6 falsification screen, replays Vaughan's identity exactly, and refutes separate Goldbach Type II envelope bounds at N=930,930 while retaining the joint signed cancellation target

release_trail.title: Cutoff frontier splits the two prime routes
release_trail.measure: 39 cutoff pairs / 0 balanced Goldbach survivors / 4 balanced Twin survivors / 0 energy failures
release_trail.proof: TICKET-101 rejects near-square-root decomposition collapse, retains a joint balanced Goldbach target, and identifies the noncollapsed Twin U=100,V=84 separated-budget candidate while classifying energy rewrites as equivalent

release_trail.title: Twin threshold corrected on a fresh dyadic holdout
release_trail.measure: 1M registered-budget failures at 2M / 4M fresh points at 8M / 0 rescue failures
release_trail.proof: TICKET-102 refutes the selected K_S=1.40 envelope, proves that any fixed finite K/log constant would be sufficient, and preserves K_S=4,K_II=1 only as fresh finite evidence

release_trail.title: Twin cumulative masking removed
release_trail.measure: 7 local blocks through 8M / Type II sign counterexample at X=1000 / 0 contract failures
release_trail.proof: TICKET-103 recomputes exact correlations on (X/2,X], refutes universal local Type II nonnegativity, and retains only an eventual fixed-finite-budget theorem

release_trail.title: Twin Type II reduced to weighted Möbius cancellation
release_trail.measure: 4 exact anatomy rows / Abel K up to 1088 / 0 identity failures
release_trail.proof: TICKET-104 derives T_X=sum mu(d)A_X(d) with nonnegative shifted-prime weights and rejects negative-mass and Abel-triangle templates that discard the decisive weight-sign correlation

release_trail.title: Twin progression main centered out
release_trail.measure: 4 centered rows / negative-mass K down to 5.41 at 2M / 0 identity failures
release_trail.proof: TICKET-105 subtracts the independent coprime progression mean, sharply reduces termwise envelopes, and isolates the Möbius-weighted prime-progression discrepancy requiring dispersion

release_trail.title: Sparse-modulus Twin leakage isolated
release_trail.measure: 72.31% one-sample support at 2M / 96% centered mass / 0 identity failures
release_trail.proof: TICKET-106 groups actual moduli before norms and shows the positive centered signal is dominated by row-unique sparse progression cells, forcing dense dispersion and sparse-tail control to separate

release_trail.title: Twin sparse tail recombined at integer level
release_trail.measure: 8M / 1.59M q to 1.10M n / 69.53% L1 retained / 0 contract failures
release_trail.proof: TICKET-107 combines occupancy-one moduli at their actual n values, verifies the vector against an independent Vaughan decomposition, and shows that structured, sparse, and dense signed compensation must survive the final estimate

release_trail.title: Twin joint restatement rejected and bump bridge opened
release_trail.measure: 8M / hard equivalence error below 4.2e-9 / smoothing improves 2 of 6 blocks / 0 contract failures
release_trail.proof: TICKET-108 proves the fully recombined hard-cutoff target is the original residual theorem, discards that circular reduction, and replaces it with a fixed nonnegative bump whose excess over explicit contamination is sufficient for twin-prime infinitude

release_trail.title: Twin single-origin spectral route refuted
release_trail.measure: 1.05M / 4 of 4 low-frequency bounds refuted / phase ratio 0.1435 / 0 contract failures
release_trail.proof: TICKET-109 verifies the exact shift-two Fourier phase identity and shows that origin-centered low-frequency energy misses the rational arithmetic structure needed for a positive lower bound

release_trail.title: Twin rational major arcs isolated the minor saving
release_trail.measure: 1.05M / Q=32 major 461.2K / minor -2.1K / trivial minor -3.11M / 0 failures
release_trail.proof: TICKET-110 fixes rational arc masks before reading target contributions, reconstructs the observed correlation, and isolates a signed Type II minor-arc power saving as the missing theorem

release_trail.title: Twin Type II phase obligation isolated
release_trail.measure: 2.10M holdout / phase-blind -6.67M / X^-1/6 finite lower +257.8K / 0 failures
release_trail.proof: TICKET-111 exactly splits the rational-minor spectrum into Vaughan Type I and Type II pieces, refutes every phase-blind partition-Cauchy refinement, and promotes a phase-aware uniform power-saving theorem

release_trail.title: Twin Farey endpoint bottleneck isolated
release_trail.measure: 162 cells / Abel envelope 1.28M / 96.05% endpoint loss / finite inherited lower +770.0K
release_trail.proof: TICKET-112 uses exact cellwise Abel summation to remove 83.15% of the phase-blind envelope, refutes independent endpoint triangles, and isolates uniform endpoint cancellation as the next theorem

release_trail.title: Twin Farey denominator blocks survive a new holdout
release_trail.measure: 4.19M holdout / 162 endpoints to 31 blocks / grouped lower +1.017M / 0 identity failures
release_trail.proof: TICKET-113 freezes canonical right-boundary-denominator grouping, retains 35.52% of endpoint envelope at 4M, and uses a magnitude-label adversary to show that a uniform Vaughan phase theorem is still indispensable

release_trail.title: Twin Ramanujan mean separated from numerator dispersion
release_trail.measure: 4.19M / 31 denominator blocks / sign-free lower +327.95K / adverse budget 82.50% / 0 contract failures
release_trail.proof: TICKET-114 proves the exact Ramanujan-mean and centered-numerator decomposition, proves its projected L2 support bound is sharp under the weak contract, and isolates an eventually subcritical Vaughan numerator-dispersion estimate as the missing theorem

release_trail.title: Twin complex mean direction separated from centering
release_trail.measure: 4.19M / scalar-aware lower +335.52K / orientation-free lower +248.13K / 6 of 6 weak-contract regressions
release_trail.proof: TICKET-115 proves the exact half-Farey complex mean decomposition, retains a modest scalar-aware improvement, and refutes orientation-free complex extraction as a stronger proof contract

release_trail.title: Twin Möbius sign interaction kept before norms
release_trail.measure: 4.19M / 1.65M outer pairs / independent budget 2.889x / independent lower -2.402M / 0 contract failures
release_trail.proof: TICKET-116 lifts the endpoint coefficients into exact positive and negative outer-Möbius layers, proves the centered polarization identity, and refutes independent sign-layer triangles while retaining a signed Vaughan dispersion target

release_trail.title: Twin signed dyadic Gram localizes a near-closing adjacent-pair budget
release_trail.measure: 4.19M / 8 dyadic blocks / singleton 1.605x / adjacent-pair lower -1.24K / 0 contract failures
release_trail.proof: TICKET-117 proves the exact signed dyadic endpoint Gram identity, rejects singleton and denominator-Cauchy closure, and localizes 51.8% of the paired budget in the first low-divisor shell pair

release_trail.title: Twin canonical adjacent pairs pass a preregistered 8M holdout
release_trail.measure: 8.39M / preregistered commit 5b52d4d / canonical budget 1.194x signed / finite lower +156.73K
release_trail.proof: TICKET-118 freezes one denominator-independent adjacent-shell rule before evaluation, obtains one positive finite holdout without retuning, and keeps the eventual signed bilinear theorem open

evidence_flow.evidence: 10 task templates; 10 lint fixtures pass expectations; 0 submitted artifacts
public_demo_only; real-world and Bitcoin attribution blocked

hardening_map.risk: Submitted provenance can name the wrong baseline or library.
hardening_map.guard: provenance_record must match task baseline_id and library.

hardening_map.risk: A feature vector can be labelled as a different generator family.
hardening_map.guard: feature_vector_summary.label must match the task library.

hardening_map.risk: Top-level baseline/library fields can contradict the task.
hardening_map.guard: Optional record identity fields must match the task when present.

hardening_map.risk: A public artifact can leak local paths, URLs, or parent traversal.
hardening_map.guard: feature_vector_path must be a public relative path.

hardening_map.risk: Lint behavior can drift after contract changes.
hardening_map.guard: 10 public-safe fixtures replay pass/warn/block boundaries before intake.

evidence_spine.proof: The original browser experiment is now backed by larger reproducible local snapshots.

evidence_spine.gate: 48 grid rows, 5,000 null iterations, 8 replication settings
evidence_spine.proof: Generator-fingerprint claims are limited to controlled synthetic evidence that survives confound checks.

evidence_spine.gate: 10 targets, 10 public-safe fixtures, 0 accepted real submissions
evidence_spine.proof: OpenSSL/BoringSSL/Go/Bitcoin collection is specified and lint-tested, but real aggregate baselines are not accepted yet.

evidence_spine.gate: 0 accepted baselines, 4 provenance rows blocked, 9,028 P0 samples projected
evidence_spine.proof: The project now states exactly why stronger real-world claims remain blocked.

evidence_spine.gate: 21 checked artifacts, 15 gates, 24 lineage nodes, 11 guard checks
evidence_spine.proof: Public statements are constrained by claim-language audit, checksums, lineage, decision rules, falsification guards, and consistency checks.

evidence_spine.gate: TICKET82 refutes every exponent-independent Mersenne compensation window by explicit infinite exponent progressions.
evidence_spine.proof: The proof workbench now targets a genuinely growing exponent-dependent descent window L(k).

latest_changes.label: Scale evidence
latest_changes.impact: The browser demo is now backed by 10M live compute plus static 1M/10M snapshots.

latest_changes.label: Controlled signal
latest_changes.impact: Synthetic generator fingerprints must survive bit-length controls, null calibration, and replication before they count.

latest_changes.label: Real-world gate
latest_changes.impact: OpenSSL/BoringSSL/Go/Bitcoin targets are registered, but attribution remains blocked until accepted aggregate baselines arrive.

latest_changes.label: Submission discipline
latest_changes.impact: Collection contract, lint fixtures, and intake validation define exactly what public-safe evidence must contain.

latest_changes.label: Publication guardrail
latest_changes.impact: Claim-language audit, claim ledger, lineage, decision rules, falsification checks, and consistency checks keep the public page at demo/scaffold claim strength.

latest_changes.label: Proof route pruning
latest_changes.impact: TICKET-46 refutes all five tested Collatz scalar clause-rank grammars at 28 bits, so the remaining target is an ordinal/stateful infinite bridge.

latest_changes.label: Stateful lasso pruning
latest_changes.impact: TICKET-47 refutes bounded suffix-memory repairs on a 16-edge positive-debt pressure lasso; arbitrary automaton CEGIS and reachability remain open.

latest_changes.label: Automaton reachability split
latest_changes.impact: TICKET-48 refutes fixed finite total state repairs over the abstract lasso and shows the bounded concrete probe reaches only two positive steps, not a full lasso period.

latest_changes.label: Preimage obstruction isolated
latest_changes.impact: TICKET-49 classifies the first concrete lasso-prefix failure as a next_valuation mismatch: the unique two-step survivor reaches 5 where the lasso requires 1.

latest_changes.label: Phase-lift exception promoted
latest_changes.impact: TICKET-50 refutes the TICKET-49 all-phase obstruction candidate at 32 bits and promotes two depth-15 near-lasso residues as the next terminal-lift targets.

latest_changes.label: Terminal lift closed
latest_changes.impact: TICKET-51 opens all low/high terminal branches for the two TICKET-50 near-lasso roots and finds zero full-lasso completions.

latest_changes.label: 48-bit frontier exposed
latest_changes.impact: TICKET-52 quantifies the 48-bit valuation frontier, finds one new sampled depth-15 near-lasso root outside the closed ancestry, and terminally closes it.

latest_changes.label: Terminal theorem isolated
latest_changes.impact: TICKET-53 turns the repeated phase-15 failure into a symbolic low/high branch mismatch theorem for the extracted lasso family.

latest_changes.label: Next family extracted
latest_changes.impact: TICKET-54 removes the TICKET53 terminal family and shows the exact 32-bit post-discard frontier drops to max depth 5 at the Phase5ValuationGate.

latest_changes.label: Gate tunnel closed
latest_changes.impact: TICKET-55 shows every known phase-5 gate crosser preserves the same pending valuation through phase 14 and then hits the TICKET53 terminal no-go.

latest_changes.label: Projection escape isolated
latest_changes.impact: TICKET-56 closes the exact 32-bit start-template lasso partition, then discards simple projection closure because a sampled 48-bit depth-15 witness projects outside the 32-bit model.

latest_changes.label: Boundary state obstruction
latest_changes.impact: TICKET-57 shows the exact32 partition is not governed by template-only automata: 2^26 boundary still has 92 collisions, while 2^28 first becomes deterministic and known roots have 0 full-period replays.

latest_changes.label: Lift stability refuted
latest_changes.impact: TICKET-58 replays the deterministic 48-bit sample and shows unchanged exact32 affine-boundary lifting fails: 3,086 projection escapes and 70 projection-target outcome mismatches.

latest_changes.label: Cylinder coordinate gap
latest_changes.impact: TICKET-59 exhaustively enumerates 162 selected low40-to-48 cylinders, strengthening the mismatch evidence while showing low40 leaves 58 mixed-outcome cylinders.

latest_changes.label: Failure-offset separator
latest_changes.impact: TICKET-60 shows low40 plus failure_offset deterministically separates the selected mixed cylinders, turning the next target into a symbolic predictor for that offset.

latest_changes.label: Pre-replay separator
latest_changes.impact: TICKET-61 forbids replay-derived keys and finds low40 plus high_extension mod 16 predicts failure_offset, outcome, and boundary-match status on the selected mixed cylinders.

latest_changes.label: Mod16 lift survival
latest_changes.impact: TICKET-62 lifts the selected mixed rows to 52 and 56 bits and finds no mod16 collision or full-period replay among surviving start-template lifts.

latest_changes.label: Mod16 automaton table
latest_changes.impact: TICKET-63 chains the 56-bit survivors to 60 bits, keeps 209 start-template target rows, and finds deterministic state tables with no audited automaton collisions.

latest_changes.label: Symbolic gate obstruction
latest_changes.impact: TICKET-64 extends the 60-bit survivors to 64-bit candidate children and refutes the direct 0->0 symbolic transition shortcut.

latest_changes.label: Start-template chain extinction
latest_changes.impact: TICKET-65 follows the retained start-template chain through 80 bits and finds the survivor sequence 824->209->42->12->3->1->0, while compact gate separators collapse to row-unique states.

latest_changes.label: Complement frontier isolated
latest_changes.impact: TICKET-66 tests the missing complement cover and finds 17,134 open needs_split instances across 491 template families, so the next target is a rank theorem or compatible infinite lift.

latest_changes.label: Rank cycle frontier
latest_changes.impact: TICKET-67 splits the 491 families one more level and refutes simple rank closure by exposing a 429-node cyclic SCC and 96,433 nondecreasing debt edges.

latest_changes.label: Refined DAG frontier
latest_changes.impact: TICKET-68 tests seven refinements of the 429-node SCC; prefix/consumed coordinates make the observed graph acyclic, while tail/residue-only refinement still leaves 26 cyclic nodes.

latest_changes.label: Rank frontier audit
latest_changes.impact: TICKET-69 checks every observed internal prefix/consumed edge and finds zero rank nondecrease, but leaves 6,649 rank-0 child-only states to expand.

latest_changes.label: Frontier closure refuted
latest_changes.impact: TICKET-70 expands the rank-0 frontier and shows direct terminal closure fails: 155,321 branches re-enter known ranks without descent and 59,449 enter new unranked internal states.

latest_changes.label: Bounded separator tradeoff
latest_changes.impact: TICKET-71 finds that a full-word residue coordinate separates the bounded branch set, while the compact coordinate keeps a smaller frontier but leaves 22,219 mixed transition keys.

latest_changes.label: Persistent lift-chain pressure
latest_changes.impact: TICKET-72 corrects rank descent out of the pressure count, then shows the remaining open pressure re-enters compact mixed keys under second and third lifts.

latest_changes.label: Strict re-entry tree exhausted
latest_changes.impact: TICKET-73 replaces capped persistence with an all-root exact lineage tree and closes the selected strict re-entry predicate at the fifth lift, without globalizing the result.

latest_changes.label: Coverage leakage exposed
latest_changes.impact: TICKET-74 shows that TICKET-73 strict extinction is not pressure closure: the fifth lift escapes the old cover and the escape forest retains open pressure at the sixth lift.

latest_changes.label: Finite-coordinate closure blocked
latest_changes.impact: TICKET-75 tests eight fixed finite pre-outcome coordinates and finds a compression-versus-state-growth trade-off instead of a closed induction invariant.

latest_changes.label: Boundary recurrence isolated
latest_changes.impact: TICKET-76 derives the exact four-bit quotient recurrence and shows why fixed low-bit repairs lose four new bits at every unresolved lift.

latest_changes.label: Fixed-prefix ghost classified
latest_changes.impact: Corrected TICKET-77 retains equality rollback, classifies the periodic quotient orbit, and rejects its -1/3 preimage as a positive-integer counterexample.

latest_changes.label: Finite 2-adic separator blocked
latest_changes.impact: TICKET-78 proves every finite accelerated valuation cylinder has infinitely many natural witnesses and rejects continuous finite-state natural classifiers.

latest_changes.label: Bounded coupled rank blocked
latest_changes.impact: TICKET-79 refutes every fixed log-height coefficient plus bounded finite-state correction as a universal one-step Collatz rank.

latest_changes.label: Finite least-counterexample compactness blocked
latest_changes.impact: TICKET-80 shows finite non-descent prefixes remain satisfiable above every finite bound and their nested all-ones witnesses converge 2-adically to -1.

research_delta.headline: What changed from the original prime-regularity demo to the current research scaffold.

claim_state.before: Exploratory prime regularity visualization with synthetic generator intuition.
claim_state.current: Controlled synthetic fingerprint evidence is null-calibrated and replication-audited, while real-world and Bitcoin attribution remain gated.
claim_state.next: Accepted OpenSSL/BoringSSL/Go baselines plus labelled classifier vectors are required before real-world attribution claims can move.

tracks.current: 48-row attribution grid, 5,000 null iterations, 8-setting replication audit, 12 classifier vectors
5 registered baseline families, 10 collection targets, 10 handoff tasks, 10 submission templates, pre-intake lint, 10 fixture cases, 10 intake blockers, 0 accepted baselines
21 checksummed artifacts, claim-language audit, open-problem certificates, claim ledger, lineage DAG, decision protocol, falsification battery, consistency audit

tracks.before: Scalar rank and symbolic clause candidates could still look plausible on bounded horizons.
tracks.current: TICKET-46 refutes 5/5 tested finite template-local scalar clause-rank grammars; TICKET-47 refutes bounded suffix-memory repairs; TICKET-48 refutes fixed finite total state repairs over the abstract lasso; TICKET-49 localizes the first concrete prefix failure to next_valuation; TICKET-50 refutes that obstruction's all-phase extension; TICKET-51 closes the two known depth-15 near-lasso roots at phase 15; TICKET-52 finds a new sampled 48-bit depth-15 root and closes it; TICKET-53 explains all known terminal closures by a symbolic mismatch theorem and discards the extracted lasso family; TICKET-54 extracts Phase5ValuationGate as the strongest remaining bounded family; TICKET-55 proves the known gate crossers tunnel into the terminal no-go; TICKET-56 closes the exact 32-bit partition but refutes simple projection-closure globalization; TICKET-57 shows exact32 outcomes need affine boundary coordinates; TICKET-58 refutes unchanged exact32 affine-boundary lifting in the replayed 48-bit sample; TICKET-59 counts selected low40-to-48 cylinders and shows the next missing coordinate is inside mixed cylinders; TICKET-60 identifies replay-derived failure_offset as a separator; TICKET-61 replaces it with the pre-replay coordinate low40 + high_extension mod 16; TICKET-62 tests 52/56-bit lifts and finds no mod16 collision among surviving start-template lifts; TICKET-63 extracts deterministic automaton state tables and reduces the chained 60-bit quotient to low40 mod 2^20 + base_mod16; TICKET-64 refutes the direct 0->0 symbolic transition shortcut at 64 bits; TICKET-65 closes the tracked start-template chain by 80 bits; TICKET-66 refutes the already-covered complement shortcut; TICKET-67 refutes one-step split closure, strict template rank, and scalar debt rank; TICKET-68 breaks the observed 429-node SCC under prefix/consumed refinement; TICKET-69 validates strict rank descent on the observed internal edges and moves the proof gap to the 6,649-state unexpanded frontier; TICKET-70 expands that frontier and refutes direct rank-0 terminal closure; TICKET-71 finds a bounded full-word separator but leaves compact infinite lift closure open; TICKET-72 separates rank descent from open pressure and exposes persistent mixed-key lift-chain pressure; TICKET-73 exactly extinguishes the selected strict re-entry tree at the fifth lift, while leaving root coverage open; TICKET-74 proves that this strict extinction leaks into new unranked pressure states and cannot be promoted to a global cover.

claim_lanes.basis: controlled grid + null + replication + scoped classifier
accepted baselines and classifier vectors missing
nonce-risk report and wallet baseline metadata missing

phases.label: Prime regularity plan
phases.summary: Defined the practical target as generator traces and anomaly detection, not raw prime prediction.

phases.label: Interactive Conjecture Lab
phases.summary: Built browser-side comparison of rejection, next_prime, and wheel observation measures up to 10M.

phases.label: 10M research snapshots
phases.summary: Bundled local precomputed SVG snapshots so GitHub Pages can show larger runs without recomputing.

phases.label: Bitcoin defensive track
phases.summary: Separated public secp256k1 constants from practical nonce and signature metadata risk.

phases.label: Generator fingerprint baseline
phases.summary: Added residue, low-bit, and local prime-gap fingerprints with baseline distance comparison.

phases.label: Controlled attribution grid
phases.summary: Added paired uncontrolled and bit-length-controlled experiments with repeat confidence intervals.

phases.label: Null calibration
phases.summary: Calibrates controlled attribution profiles against a row-structured random-label null with family-wise profile selection control.

phases.label: Replication audit
phases.summary: Audits whether null-calibrated controlled profiles replicate across limit, train-count, and test-count settings.

phases.label: Crypto-classifier baseline
phases.summary: Exports fixed-length controlled synthetic feature vectors and runs a nearest-centroid classifier while keeping real-world attribution gated.

phases.label: Real-world baseline registry
phases.summary: Registered OpenSSL, BoringSSL, Go, Bitcoin Core, and wallet/library families while keeping sensitive raw data local.

phases.label: Real-world collection matrix
phases.summary: Defines matched 2048/3072/4096-bit RSA and Bitcoin signature metadata targets before claims can advance.

phases.label: Sample power calibration
phases.summary: Estimates conservative TV-drift screening floors so sample counts are not treated as stronger evidence than they support.

phases.label: Provenance requirements
phases.summary: Defines required metadata and forbidden public fields before real-world samples can support attribution claims.

phases.label: Provenance audit
phases.summary: Audits filled provenance records for missing metadata, checksum format, and forbidden public sensitive fields before attribution claims.

phases.label: Baseline acceptance
phases.summary: Combines collection availability, provenance audit, and sample-power tier before a real-world baseline can be used for attribution claims.

phases.label: Promotion plan
phases.summary: Identifies the minimal OpenSSL/BoringSSL-style unlock path and required sample floor before accepted real-world evidence is possible.

phases.label: Collection handoff
phases.summary: Converts baseline gates into prioritized public-safe collection tasks with provenance, sample floor, and classifier-scope constraints.

phases.label: Submission contract
phases.summary: Exports machine-readable public submission templates that bind task IDs, optional record identity, public feature-vector paths, sample floors, checksums, provenance identity, feature vectors, and forbidden private fields.

phases.label: Submission lint
phases.summary: Runs pre-intake checks over candidate public submission records so collectors can fix contract, checksum, feature-vector, duplicate, and public-safety errors before claim gates.

phases.label: Fixture audit
phases.summary: Runs public-safe pass, warning, missing-feature, forbidden-field, provenance-identity, feature-label, record-identity, feature-path, and reused-checksum fixtures against the submission linter before real collector data is accepted.

phases.label: Collection intake
phases.summary: Validates submitted aggregate artifacts against handoff tasks before they can become accepted real-world baselines.

phases.label: Research readiness scoring
phases.summary: Scores Sim-to-Real, attribution validation, classifier data, and Bitcoin integration before allowing stronger claims.

phases.label: Evidence pack gates
phases.summary: Bundles artifact checksums, publication gates, claim level, and local collection protocols.

phases.label: Claim-language audit
phases.summary: Scans README, docs, and GitHub Pages copy so public wording cannot exceed the current evidence boundary.

phases.label: Claim ledger
phases.summary: Maps each public-facing claim to the gates and artifacts that allow, qualify, or block it.

phases.label: Artifact lineage
phases.summary: Builds an acyclic dependency graph over public artifacts and checks evidence-pack checksums against current files.

phases.label: Decision protocol
phases.summary: Applies pre-registered publication and claim-promotion rules to evidence, claim, and lineage reports.

phases.label: Falsification battery
phases.summary: Runs negative-control, bit-length confound, and claim-promotion checks before attribution claims can be upgraded.

phases.label: Publication consistency
phases.summary: Checks that evidence, claim, decision, and falsification artifacts agree on the same high-risk claim boundary.

open_gaps.gap: Need at least two available aggregate generator baselines before real attribution claims.
Need labelled feature vectors across OpenSSL, BoringSSL, Go, and suspicious samples.
Need a bundled Bitcoin nonce-risk report from owned or public metadata summaries.
